units.title: Medicine as Both a Science and an Art
units.titleZh: 医学既是科学也是艺术

units.title: Landmark Cases in Bioethics
units.titleZh: 生命伦理学里程碑案例

items.example: Academic clinicians need to challenge the status quo through the pursuit of excellence in practice, teaching, and research.
By observing experienced healthcare professionals, medical students learn to apply their theoretical understanding in a real-world setting, which fosters critical thinking and problem-solving skills.
From a regulatory perspective, trials should be judged based on how they generate adequate data for the benefit/risk assessment.
Mindset, nutrition, activity, and rest are four pillars of enhanced recovery from the patient's perspective.
Negative emotions may affect people's decisions and actions in implementing effective disease-prevention measures.
The do-no-harm principle is sometimes considered an element of beneficence, and it is described as the first rule of medicine.
The increasing number of health and medical institutions offering Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) services has greatly enhanced the quality and efficacy of TCM-related services in China.
The justice approach to ethical decision-making asserts that everyone should be fairly treated.
The new guideline includes recommendations on the perioperative management of patients who are undergoing minor procedures.
There is no consensus on how to best address the issue of rising healthcare costs.
This study aims to assess the current state of the doctor-patient relationship to generate baseline evidence for policy discussions.
Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) is one of the oldest forms of personalized medicine and uses a different paradigm from Western medicine.
Bloodletting had a rational basis since local inflammation was easily interpreted as evidence that the body had too much blood, and therefore needed aid in ridding itself of it.
Herbal remedies have a long history of use, ultimately contributing to modern pharmacology.
If holism attracts modern healers to the Greek, there are other complementary attributes to Hippocratic medicine that resonate within contemporary scientific medicine.
Medical archaeology helps us trace ancient medical practices through artifacts and writings.
Medical technology has facilitated surgeries and diagnostics in ways unimaginable in the past.
The accumulation of scientific data continues to drive forward our understanding of the human body and its ailments.
The development of vaccines has been a revolutionary milestone in preventing deadly diseases.
The discovery of antibiotics intensified the fight against infectious diseases.
The enabling effect of the printing press revolutionized the dissemination of medical knowledge in the Renaissance.
The history of medicine is marked by the dynamic evolution of medical practices and knowledge.
The increased levels of local violence and population decline north of the Alps from perhaps 12 million in 200 to about 10 million in 600 must have made for some grim moments in many lives.
The phenomena of epidemics have shaped the course of medical history, prompting advances in public health.
Doctors rely on comprehensive screenings to accurately assess a medical patient's overall health and identify potential health issues.
Cognitive therapy in psychiatry aims to transform the negative thought patterns that contribute to anxiety and depression, promoting better mental health.
The healthcare industry is undergoing a dynamic shift towards digital engagement, with telehealth usage remaining 11 times higher than before.
Healthcare professionals are expected to uphold the highest standards of honesty and ethical behavior, which are the cornerstones of integrity.
Understanding the intricate mechanism of the human immune system is vital in developing effective treatments for various diseases.
Despite the risks associated with surgery, the patient's determination to improve their quality of life, nevertheless, led them to proceed with the life-saving operation.
In medicine, it is important to acknowledge the patient's views about their condition and its treatment.
Doctors must prioritize their treatment plan to ensure that urgent interventions are addressed first.
Timely and accurate diagnosis is crucial to enable healthcare professionals to provide effective treatment and improve patient outcomes.
The introduction of electronic health records has led to a transformation in the way healthcare providers store, access, and share patient information.
Traditional Chinese Medicine emphasizes the restoration of balance within the body, targeting the intrinsic energy flows known as qi for holistic healing.
Promoting patient wellness and delivering high-quality care is the ultimate goal of healthcare providers.

Access to health care is a fundamental aspect of biomedical ethics, ensuring that individuals have the opportunity to receive necessary medical treatment regardless of their background or socioeconomic status.
Careful consideration of potential risks and benefits should be anticipated before conducting any research involving human participants, ensuring their safety and well-being are prioritized.
In biomedical researches, researchers must not assume consent from participants but rather obtain explicit informed consent before involving them in any study or clinical trial.
Informed consent is coupled with comprehensive information about potential risks and benefits to ensure that research participants can make well-informed decisions about their involvement.

The distributive justice principle guides the fair allocation of limited medical resources, ensuring that healthcare services and treatments are distributed equitably among individuals and communities based on their needs and circumstances.

Biomedical researches rely on empirical evidence to inform decision-making processes, ensuring that ethical considerations are grounded in scientific research and objective data rather than speculation.
A robust ethical framework is essential in biomedical research to provide guidelines and principles that govern the conduct of studies.
Biomedical ethics encourages researchers to incorporate principles of beneficence, non-maleficence, and respect for autonomy into their study designs.
Clear and transparent procedures for obtaining informed consent, conducting research, and handling data are essential to uphold ethical standards and protect the well-being of research participants.
The ratio of potential benefits to risks is carefully evaluated when considering whether to proceed with a research study involving human participants.
Conflicts of interest and ethical dilemmas are carefully resolved through transparent discussions and consensus-building among relevant stakeholders.
Adhering too rigidly to established guidelines and protocols can sometimes overlook unique ethical considerations in certain situations.
3D-printed robotic hands have been used to restore the functions after critical hand injuries.
Allergens inside an individual's body trigger the defense mechanism of the person to produce antibodies.
Bacteria can be modified to prevent them from causing infectious disease and then used as vectors to carry therapeutic genes into human tissues.
Children diagnosed with diabetes during infancy have unique clinical characteristics.
Clinicians might consider the routine initiation of anti-depressants even when there is no explicit evidence for psychological distress.
Humans are capable of learning novel concepts from very few examples; in contrast, state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms typically need thousands of examples to do so.
Researchers should ensure that all team members under their supervision are trained to conduct ethically acceptable research.
The gathered data have been used to prioritize the symptoms and/or diseases that herbal medicine may have the best potential to treat.
The need for a more effective allocation of the healthcare resources through the use of digital health innovation became evident.
Tuberculosis can be transmitted between human and animals.
When the body encounters allergens, the immune system will start off a series of reactions to eliminate them.
Wireless remote monitoring (RM) has fundamentally changed the paradigm of how physicians manage their patients.
By voluntarily participating in genetic research, individuals can contribute to scientific advancement while ensuring their rights and privacy are protected.
Each individual's genetic makeup is unique and deserving of respect.
Enormous potential exists for genetic engineering to improve human health.
Explicit ethical guidelines are crucial for responsible genetic testing.
Genetic intervention raises profound ethical questions about the boundaries of human manipulation.
Open communication and public engagement are crucial for addressing the ambiguity surrounding genetic testing and promoting informed decision-making.
Qualitative research helps illuminate the human experience of genetic testing.
Strict international regulations prohibit human germline editing, aiming to prevent unintended consequences.
The erosion of trust in scientific institutions due to past ethical lapses necessitates transparent communication regarding genetic research.
The prospect of genetic manipulation raises profound ethical concerns.
The ideological perspectives surrounding the potential for genetic testing to exacerbate social divisions need careful ethical attention.
Unanticipated side effects of CRISPR gene editing sparked discussions about the technology's safety.
Hospice teams build a strong foundation of comfort, dignity, and emotional well-being during end-of-life journeys by providing compassionate care.
The hospice team works closely with patients and their families to help them anticipate and navigate the challenges of end-of-life care.
Mental conditions such as depression are carefully attended to in palliative care to improve patient's quality of life.
Following strictly doctor's prescription and taking medications regularly can help patients attain a significant reduction in their blood pressure levels.
In emergency medicine, the experienced practitioner quickly assesses and triages patients based on the severity of their condition.
After a thorough examination and consultation, the medical team decided to proceed with a surgery.
The hospital provides a comfortable and peaceful environment in the terminal stage of patients' lives.
Through careful assessment and personalized treatment plans, doctors are able to determine the most effective interventions for their patients' conditions.
Providing effective end-of-life care requires a deep understanding of palliative medicine and a specialized expertise in managing pain and other symptoms.
It is imperative for healthcare providers to be prepared to address the dramatic emotional changes and needs that may arise during palliative care.
Public health officials are constantly analyzing data and forecasting potential scenarios to effectively manage and respond to the spread of resurgent diseases.
It is essential to consider the ethical implications and ensure the patient's wishes are respected when making decisions about end-of-life care.
Healthcare professionals demonstrate their adaptability by swiftly adjusting to new medical technologies.
Effective assessment skills are essential for healthcare professionals to accurately diagnose and treat patients.
Medical professionals should adhere to ethical codes of conduct, ensuring their actions align with principles of integrity and respect for patient autonomy.
Consistency in applying evidence-based practices can foster trust among medical professionals and patients.
Healthcare professionals should resist the influence of financial incentives that may compromise patient care.
Effective patient care not only incorporates medical expertise but also ethical considerations.
Medical professionals need to be enrolled in comprehensive orientation programs so that their knowledge and skills get enhanced.
Addressing patients' perceived needs and concerns needs to be prioritized to enhance the caregiver-patient relationship.
Professionalism is the cornerstone of healthcare practice, encompassing qualities such as integrity, compassion and accountability.
Medical professionals employ rational decision making process based on evidence-based diagnosis.
Medical professionals should acknowledge the importance of undergoing regular self-reflection and peer review processes of their clinical practice.
Physician burnout can present variably in medical professionals' clinical practice and daily life.
Aquatic therapy reduces the motion and stress barriers, making it a great alternative to traditional treatment for people with mobility issues and pain.
If patients do not verbalize clearly, their intentions may be wrongly interpreted by the care providers.
It is difficult to achieve a balance between doing everything that is technically feasible and ethically appropriate to improve quality of care at the end of life.
Many new digital devices have been designed to monitor blood pressure remotely, including smart watches and fitness trackers with built-in blood pressure tracking capabilities.
Personalized medicine has promised to radically change the way we look at health and disease.
Physicians' integrity forms a foundation for patients' trust and fosters healthy therapeutic relationships that promote healing.
Technology helps to make health information more accessible to the general public.
Telecommunication technology in the healthcare industry has completely transformed the paradigm of delivery of healthcare services.
The analysis did not yield any compelling evidence to support the hypothesis, making the statement inconclusive.
The doctor prioritizes open communication with their patients and likewise expects the same level of honesty from them.
The rising investments in research and development shall enhance the efficiency and reliability of the therapies.
Whether for patients themselves or their family members, specific coping strategies are needed to manage or alter the source of stress or to regulate stressful emotions.
Equipping patients with the knowledge and tools to manage their health empowers them to take an active role in their care.
Ethical considerations are essential in navigating complex medical decisions.
Every patient encounter is an opportunity to learn and grow as a clinician.
Narrative medicine advocates for a patient-centered approach to health care.
Ongoing communication and follow-up are key in ensuring continuity of care.
Patients who undergo complex medical procedures often require additional emotional support.
Proceeding with a medical intervention without fully informing the patient of the risks and benefits is a violation of ethical principles.
The hierarchy of power within the medical system can greatly impact the way patient stories are heard and valued.
The intense emotions and challenges that come with a medical diagnosis can greatly impact a patient's well-being.
The study of psychology helps healthcare professionals understand the behavioral and emotional aspects of their patients' experiences.
Textual analysis of medical records can provide valuable information about a patient's medical history.
Visual aids such as diagrams and models can help patients better understand their medical condition.

items.meaning: 医学干预的疗效/有效性
专家/经验丰富的临床医生
实践/一般/个人/默会知识
涉及人体受试者的研究

items.term: ratio of risks to anticipated benefits

items.meaning: 具有法律约束力的条约

items.term: be open to changes and improvements

items.meaning: 披露 HIV 阳性状态
逻辑和理性的问题解决

items.term: ally in the fight for healthcare justice
items.meaning: 医疗正义斗争中的盟友

items.term: A basic principle of ... is to do sth
items.meaning: 辐射安全的基本原则是保持尽可能低的暴露量。
items.example: A basic principle of radiation safety is to keep exposure as low as reasonably achievable.

items.meaning: “优雅地老去”这句话我们听过多少次？
items.example: How often have we heard the saying, "to age gracefully"?

items.meaning: 你是否考虑过这些饮料如何影响你的牙齿？让我们从基础知识开始，了解液体茶饮是如何影响我们牙齿的。
items.example: Have you ever considered how these drinks affect your teeth? Let us start with the basics and learn how liquid tea affects our teeth.

items.meaning: 许多（如果不是大多数）临床测试和程序会引起疼痛和不适，尤其是在执行错误的时候。
items.example: Many, if not most, clinical tests and procedures may cause pain and discomfort, especially when mistakes are made.

items.meaning: 人们很容易忘记，身体是一台精密调节的生物机器，需要精心护理才能发挥最佳性能。
items.example: It is easily forgotten that the body is a finely tuned biological machine and needs careful maintenance to perform at its best.

items.meaning: 无论你面临何种程度的压力，锻炼总是有帮助的。
items.example: No matter to what extent you are under pressure, exercise is always helpful.

items.meaning: 不言而喻，在任何不安全的社交网络平台上共享患者身份信息都是不合适的。
items.example: It goes without saying that sharing patient-identifying information on any unsafe social networking platform is inappropriate.

items.term: There is one strand that runs through ...
items.meaning: 有一条主线贯穿于医学的各个分支：早期诊断对有效治疗的重要性。
items.example: There is one strand that runs through all branches of medicine: the importance of early diagnosis for effective treatment.

items.meaning: 患者血压明显下降意味着需要调整药物以预防并发症。
items.example: That the patient's blood pressure had dropped significantly meant that medication needed to be adjusted to prevent complications.

items.meaning: 亚历山大·弗莱明（Alexander Fleming）于1928年开创性地发现了青霉素的抗菌特性，由此产生了将青霉素用作抗生素的想法。
items.example: The idea that penicillin could be used as an antibiotic came from Alexander Fleming's pioneering discovery of its antibacterial properties in 1928.

items.term: ... not arise simply from ..., but from ...
items.meaning: 医学上的突破并不只是运气使然，而是多年潜心研究的结果。
items.example: Breakthroughs in medicine do not arise simply from luck, but from years of painstaking research.

items.meaning: 基因组学和个性化医疗在21世纪取得了前所未有的发展。
items.example: The 21st century has seen unprecedented advances in genomics and personalized medicine.

items.meaning: 随着最近医学研究的突破，针对以前无法治愈的疾病患者，出现了新的治疗选择。
items.example: In the wake of recent breakthroughs in medical research, new treatment options have emerged for patients with previously incurable diseases.

items.meaning: 因为已知汞对人体的毒性影响，现代医疗实践中对汞的使用已逐渐淡出。
items.example: The use of mercury has faded from modern medical practice because of its known toxic effects on the human body.

items.meaning: 医学上的安慰剂效应是一个让研究者持续着迷的现象。
items.example: The placebo effect in medicine is a curiosity that continues to fascinate researchers.

items.term: The primary mission of ... is to ...
items.meaning: 公共卫生诊所的首要使命是为服务不足的社区提供可及且负担得起的医疗服务。
items.example: The primary mission of public health clinics is to provide accessible and affordable medical services to underserved communities.

items.meaning: 在日益严重的抗生素耐药的情况下，明智地使用抗生素并且仅在必要时使用抗生素以防止超级细菌的出现至关重要。
items.example: In a climate of increasing antibiotic resistance, it is crucial to use antibiotics wisely and only when necessary to prevent the emergence of superbugs.

items.meaning: 只有当患者正在服用药物并对其过敏或者药物使用剂量或频率不正确时才会发生药物不良反应。
items.example: Adverse drug reactions can only occur if patients are taking medicines and are allergic to them or use an incorrect dosage or frequency.

items.term: The essential difference between ... lies in ...
items.meaning: 传统医学和替代医学之间的本质区别在于它们治疗疾病的方法和所遵循的基本原则。
items.example: The essential difference between traditional medicine and alternative medicine lies in their approaches to treating disease and the principles they follow.

items.term: The ideal for ... is to do sth.
items.meaning: 医疗从业者的理想是在决策过程中优先考虑患者的自主权。
items.example: The ideal for medical practitioners is to prioritize patients' autonomy in the decision-making process.

items.term: Polls and studies have shown that ...
items.meaning: 民意调查和研究表明患者重视保密，将其视为医疗救治的一个基本方面。
items.example: Polls and studies have shown that patients value confidentiality and regard it as a basic aspect of medical care.

items.term: ... need to be discussed and answered
items.meaning: 临终关怀的伦理困境需要真诚地讨论和回答。
items.example: Ethical dilemmas in end-of-life care need to be discussed and answered honestly.

items.term: A detailed empirical study of ... concludes that ...
items.meaning: 一项关于患者对基因检测看法的详细实证研究得出的结论是，患者强烈渴望进行全面的检测前咨询。
items.example: A detailed empirical study of patients' views on genetic testing concludes that patients strongly desire comprehensive pre-test counseling.

items.meaning: 由于对生殖过程性质的伦理担忧，体外受精的广泛使用遭到了反对。
items.example: Ethical concerns about the nature of reproduction have raised opposition to the widespread use of in vitro fertilization.

items.term: Recent advances in ... could address this need.
items.meaning: 远程医疗技术的最新进展可以解决偏远地区更好地获取医疗服务的需求。
items.example: Recent advances in telemedicine could address the need for better access to medical care in remote areas.

items.meaning: 我们必须努力制定明确的条约来公平分配医疗资源。
items.example: Efforts must be made to develop clear treaties for the fair allocation of medical resources.

items.meaning: “migraine”来源于拉丁语“hemicrania”一词，意思是“半个（hemi）”“头颅（crania）”。
items.example: The word "migraine" comes from the Latin word "hemicrania", meaning "half" (hemi) and "skull" (crania).

items.meaning: “tumor”这个词现在可以与“neoplasm”互换使用，意思是新的或异常的细胞生长。
items.example: The word "tumor" is now used interchangeably with "neoplasm", meaning new or abnormal cell growth.

items.meaning: 很明显，当患者表现良好时，医生们会很高兴。
items.example: It becomes evident that doctors are pleased when patients do well.

items.meaning: 很大程度上由于巴氏试验的使用增加，过去几十年来宫颈癌死亡率大幅下降了。
items.example: Mortality from cervical cancer has declined substantially over the past several decades, due in large part to the increased use of the Pap test.

items.meaning: 免疫疗法的进步从根本上改变了癌症的治疗模式。
items.example: Advances in immunotherapy have fundamentally altered the treatment paradigm for cancer.

items.term: ... in an ethically sound way that ...
items.meaning: 研究应以符合伦理的方式进行，以保护受访者以及整个组织的诚信。
items.example: Research should be conducted in an ethically sound way that protects the integrity of the respondents and the organization as a whole.

items.meaning: 新冠疫情凸显了公共卫生基础设施在疫情防范和应对中的关键作用。
items.example: The COVID-19 pandemic brought to the fore the key role of public health infrastructure in epidemic prevention and response.

items.meaning: 抗生素耐药细菌的出现最令人担忧，因为它有可能使救命药物失效。
items.example: The emergence of antibiotic-resistant bacteria is of most concern because it has the potential to make life-saving drugs ineffective.

items.meaning: 中草药不能随意服用，因为它们可能与处方药相互作用，引起严重的并发症。
items.example: Chinese herbal medicines should not be taken casually, given that they may interact with prescription drugs and cause serious complications.

items.meaning: 护士们无法想象自己在患者感到疼痛时，不提供止痛药物或寻求其他帮助。
items.example: Nurses cannot imagine themselves providing no pain relief or seeking no other help when patients are in pain.

items.meaning: 临床试验结果揭示了有关新型癌症免疫治疗的重要信息。
items.example: Results from clinical trials revealed important insights about a novel cancer immunotherapy.

items.meaning: 有效的患者护理要求医务人员与跨学科团队合作，制订全面的治疗计划。
items.example: Effective patient care requires healthcare professionals to work in concert with interdisciplinary teams to develop comprehensive treatment plans.

items.meaning: 与常规疗法相比，替代医学方法通常强调自然疗法和整体疗法。
items.example: Alternative medical approaches often emphasize natural and holistic therapies when compared to conventional treatments.

items.meaning: 实施个体化医疗的困难之一在于将患者的基因信息整合到临床决策中带来的挑战。
items.example: Part of the difficulty in implementing personalized medicine lies in the challenge of integrating patients' genetic information into clinical decision-making.

items.meaning: 医务人员致力于充分发挥其技能和专业知识，为患者提供最高水平的护理。
items.example: Healthcare workers are committed to using their skills and expertise to their fullest extent to provide patients with the highest level of care.

items.meaning: 在生物医学领域进行人体实验时，研究人员必须优先考虑伦理问题。
items.example: It is imperative that researchers prioritize ethical concerns when conducting human experiments in the biomedical field.

items.meaning: 调整医疗保健策略以满足老龄人口的独特需求是老年医学中始终存在的现实。
items.example: Adjusting healthcare strategies to meet the unique needs of the aging population is an ever-present reality in geriatric medicine.

items.meaning: 再生医学领域的独特之处在于，它能够利用人体自身的愈合机制。
items.example: The field of regenerative medicine is unique owing to its ability to use the body's own healing mechanisms.

items.meaning: 花时间想一想为了提供最高标准的患者护理所需要的奉献和同理心。
items.example: Think for a moment of the dedication and empathy needed to provide the highest standard of patient care.

items.meaning: 为了应对患者的心理压力和困惑，我们必须优先采取富有同情心的沟通以及共同医疗决策。
items.example: To deal with patients' psychological stress and confusion, we must prioritize compassionate communication and shared medical decision-making.

items.meaning: 本质上，医学专业精神要求我们坚持终生学习，来满足日益变化的患者需求。
items.example: By its nature, medical professionalism requires us to commit to lifelong learning in order to meet patients' changing needs.

items.meaning: 有效的医学专业精神必须结合两个方面：技术熟练和富有同情心的医患交流。
items.example: Effective medical professionalism must incorporate two dimensions: technical competence and compassionate doctor-patient communication.

items.term: ... have launched the key steps towards ...
items.meaning: 导师项目已经启动了培养医疗专业人员的技能和专业知识的关键步骤。
items.example: Mentorship programs have launched the key steps towards developing the skills and expertise of medical professionals.

items.meaning: 医疗专业人员绝不应该在道德标准方面进行妥协。
items.example: Medical professionals should by no means compromise their ethical standards.

items.term: Whether ... or ... is being discussed, ... is needed.
items.meaning: 无论是讨论预防性医护还是急性治疗，都需要对患者进行教育。
items.example: Whether preventive care or acute treatment is being discussed, patient education is needed.

items.term: ... is the most important purpose of ...
items.meaning: 继续了解自己的领域并了解最新的医学进展是临床人员继续教育的最重要目的。
items.example: Continuing to understand one's field and keep up with the latest medical advances is the most important purpose of continuing education for clinicians.

items.meaning: 有人指出，远程医疗必须遵循与身体医护相同的标准。
items.example: It has been stated that telemedicine must follow the same standards as in-person care.

items.meaning: 当患者病情复杂时，最需要有效的沟通。
items.example: Effective communication is needed most when patients' conditions are complex.

items.meaning: 提供全面医护的第一步是进行临床评估。
items.example: The first step in providing comprehensive care is to conduct a clinical assessment.

items.term: ... has been proposed as a promising alternative ... to do sth.
items.meaning: 鼻内途径已被提议作为递送药物和改善阿尔茨海默病治疗的有前途的替代方案。
items.example: The intranasal route has been proposed as a promising alternative to deliver drugs and improve the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.

items.term: Aside from ..., some authors have warned that ...
items.meaning: 除了药物的常见副作用外，一些作者警告说，这种新药还可能有增加心血管并发症的风险。
items.example: Aside from the common side effects of the drug, some authors have warned that the new drug may also increase the risk of cardiovascular complications.

items.term: One of its core missions is to ...
items.meaning: 其核心任务之一是为服务不足的社区提供便捷的医疗服务。
items.example: One of its core missions is to provide accessible medical services to underserved communities.

items.meaning: 治疗可能无法保证痊愈，但可以提高患者的生活质量。
items.example: Treatment may not guarantee a cure, but it can improve patients' quality of life.

items.term: ... as is often the case in ...
items.meaning: 就像在绝症中经常发生的那样，姑息治疗侧重症状管理和情感支持。
items.example: As is often the case in terminal illness, palliative care focuses on symptom management and emotional support.

items.meaning: 医院和当地社区中心联手组织了一次健康宣传活动。
items.example: The hospital and the local community center joined hands to organize a health promotion campaign.

items.meaning: 不对高危患者进行定期筛查，就像忽视了预防医学的一个重要方面一样。
items.example: Not screening high-risk patients regularly is like ignoring an important aspect of preventive medicine.
